description: Sanitized failure-class catalogue from the local-only raw-audio validation lane (sim2real reality check). Pattern SHAPES only: class names, generic descriptions, degradation parameters, counts, safety labels. No source URLs, no audio hashes, no verbatim transcript quotes — the source clips' speakers never consented to redistribution, and a distinctive quote is identifying. Raw audio, source manifests, and the full review note live in the Operations workspace, never this repo.

observation_run.replay: route_lines, fresh in-memory DB per clip per model, lexicon on and off
observation_run.scored_private_run: run.py --include-private: 10/10 clips, 0 unsafe captures in every mode for both models, reports redirected outside the repo

entries.description: Effortful speech and dysarthric list reading repeat short units (1-2 words) before continuing; ASR transcribes the repeats verbatim. Repo repetition guard historically required >=18 words with heavy repetition, so short repetition runs pass straight through routing.

Speech (or the capture window) stops inside a word: ASR emits word fragments at the cut point, sometimes forming a shorter real word, sometimes a non-word. Whole-word fades do not reproduce the fragment shape.
A filler interjection interrupts the utterance and the speaker restarts the opening words before completing the thought. Conversational and testimonial speech shows this constantly.

Speech-therapy exercises are counting, and local ASR renders spoken numbers as digits. Before this slice, counting lines drew generic repair choices, and a probe confirmed that a bare in-range digit spoken while choices were pending selected one and captured an intent from ambient audio. Fixed: counting sequences are a no-op in normal mode and set pending choices aside; residual risk (a single bare digit while choices are pending is indistinguishable from a deliberate selection) stays documented and mitigated upstream.

Conversational monologue and narration not addressed to Parker drew generic repair choices on effectively every line (101 of 101 routed utterances; scored form: 10/10 clips classify nuisance_choices). Selection mode then swallowed every following utterance behind the number re-prompt, including real commands (probe-confirmed). Fixed this slice: clearly-new commands/questions/dismissals escape selection mode. The deeper gap — Parker has no addressed-to-me detection — is a known deferral (wake word / addressed detection), not solvable by a text guard.
Audio begins mid-sentence; leading words are lost. Every raw window opened mid-speech.
Speech ends before the sentence completes; validated as a class, with the refinement that real cuts often land inside a word (see midword_truncation).
Effortful and filler pauses surface as ellipses and segment breaks in ASR output.

ASR substitutes plausible everyday words for domain terms and names (a condition name became a vehicle; a compound word became a kinship phrase). Same class as the known name-mangle failures; arises organically when any audio meets ASR, so the generator needs no explicit variant. Names are guarded by lexicon recipient canonicalization.

Two-speaker audio (interviewer/examiner + subject) arrives as interleaved short utterances, including bare backchannels. A text-degradation generator over single-voice TTS cannot represent cross-talk; this belongs to the deferred recliner-acoustics re-recording rig.
entries.synthetic_gap: single-voice TTS cannot produce multi-speaker cross-talk; deferred to the re-recording rig

negative_observations.note: NOT re-observed in this lane: all 20 transcripts kept unique-word ratios >= 0.69. Prior evidence for long fluent loops comes from the earlier no-transcript corpus rows only, so the generator gets no loop variant from this run (do not invent degradations reality did not show).
